chunks.text: Math and CS student Tyler Hou was named a finalist for the 2026 Hertz Fellowship, a prestigious and competitive award for doctoral students in applied sciences, mathematics, and engineering. Selected from over 1,450 applicants, Hou is one of 54 finalists advancing to the final round of interviews. The fellowship, awarded by the Fannie and John Hertz Foundation, empowers promising young researchers to pursue work with transformational impact.
chunks.question: How many 2026 finalists were named?

chunks.text: The 2026 class of Hertz Fellows will be officially announced in May. Related links include the Hertz Foundation’s announcement of the 54 finalists. The item underscores the competitiveness of the fellowship and highlights Hou’s progress to the final interview stage among a large national applicant pool.
chunks.question: When are fellows announced?
